chapter_context.summary: Proclaims to Zion: 'Arise, shine; for thy light is come', describing the nations bringing wealth, the walls rebuilt, and the Lord becoming an everlasting light.

outline.text: Light of Zion Rising; Abundance of Gentiles Flowing In
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Light of Zion Rising; Abundance of Gentiles Flowing In (60:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Chariots and Ships Bringing Wealth; Rebuilding Walls
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Chariots and Ships Bringing Wealth; Rebuilding Walls (60:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Eternal Excellency of Zion; Lord as Her Sun and Shield
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Eternal Excellency of Zion; Lord as Her Sun and Shield (60:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Glory and Future Hope
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of glory and future hope, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 60, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
